item.title: Dog poo, 16th June

Do poo on pavement outside entrance to flats 4,5,…19,20 Horselydown Mansions Lafone St

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lafone Street, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Repeated breach of 7.5T weight restriction by HGVs on Lodge Lane (day and nighttime disturbance)), 16th June
item.description:
Heavy Goods Vehicles are repeatedly using Lodge Lane in Grays despite the clearly signed 7.5 tonne weight restriction on this road. This is not an isolated issue and is happening both during the night and daytime: Between approximately 11:00pm and 2:00am, HGVs frequently pass through, causing significant disturbance. Even today at around 08:30am, multiple trucks were seen cutting through, likely attempting to avoid congestion on the A13. Lodge Lane should not be used as a cut-through route by heavy vehicles under any circumstances. The restriction is in place for a reason, yet it is being routinely ignored. The impact on residents is serious: Properties are physically shaking as HGVs pass, raising concerns about potential structural damage to homes. The noise levels—especially overnight—are excessive and are disrupting sleep and wellbeing. There is an ongoing lack of enforcement, allowing this to continue unchecked. Request: Urgent enforcement of the 7.5 tonne restriction Investigation into HGVs using Lodge Lane as a diversion route from the A13 Consideration of stronger preventative measures (e.g. enforcement cameras, clearer signage, or physical restrictions) to stop misuse Residents should not have to tolerate their homes shaking or constant disturbance because lorry drivers are ignoring clearly defined restrictions. Immediate action is required before this leads to permanent damage or a more serious incident. Thanks
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lodge Lane, Stifford Clays, Grays
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Another dog poo, 16th June
item.description:
Alas, another entitled dog owner has decided they are too important to collect their furbaby’s excrement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 103, Burbage Road, Dulwich Village, Greater London

item.title: More residential waste dumped by public bin, 16th June
item.description:
Local residents are still leaving their household bags of rubbish by the public bin on Bycroft Street all the time. This has been an issue for many months. Could someone from the council speak to the landlords of the housing in this section of Penge Lane to see if better provision of communal residential bins is needed?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bycroft Street, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 16th June
item.description:
Household items & rubbish strewn over grass & falling out of bins

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Wickham Lane, Plumstead

item.title: Tree with multiple sprouts from Trunk, 16th June
item.description:

item.title: Door and Door Frame at the side of the Candy Box, 16th June
item.description:
The above problem was previuosly reported over a week ago now and the building material rubbish remains in situ. There have been no updates to date. Please can this be removed immediately as it makes the area unsightly and will if left there cause more rubbish to be dumped at this location. Many thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Maple Crescent, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Housing officer Simon Priscilla is about housing manager who targets residence on the estate he doesn't listen to residents concerns. Hackney Council housing officer Simon Priscilla is a self-centred person who does not care about anyone else but himself and money. Discriminating against the community and travelers.., 16th June
item.description:
Simon Priscilla is a incompetent. Bad manager. Working for Hackney Council.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Homerton High Street, Hackney, London

item.title: Fly tipping junction of Eastbrook Road and shooters hill, 16th June
item.description:
Rubbish everywhere Disgusting
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Shooters Hill Road, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Health hazard - ongoing issue with overflowing residential waste, 16th June
item.description:
Once again there is rubbish scattered all over the road and pavement in front of the shops by Penge East station. I've seen this issue raised many, many times on fixmystreet but a long term solution has never been sorted out. Looks like the residents of the flats above the shops need large, lockable metal wheelie bins, not the black plastic one that quickly overflows and is ransacked by foxes every week. If you look at the photos you can see rotten food, nappies, baby clothes and more, which is an environmental hazard especially in a busy thoroughfare by a train station and shops that serve food to the public. Please can a better solution be found rather than another quick clear-up?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 7, Linden Grove, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: River shuttle bank, 16th June
item.description:
River shuttle, overgrown weeds on the bank. Been getting clean every 6 months by a chap call Andrew. To grain access to the back go though no 15 gate.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 11, Harman Drive, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
